bug_report.summary: Ctrl+Click on “View Shield Studies” no longer opens about:studies in a new tab (replaces/closes Settings tab)
steps_to_reproduce: Scroll to the Studies / Shield Studies row.
Hold Ctrl and left-click the “View Shield Studies” link.
expected_behaviors: The new tab’s URL is about:studies.
The original Settings (about:preferences#privacy) tab remains open.
actual_behaviors: about:studies opens but the original Settings tab is not preserved (Settings tab disappears), indicating the link opened in the current tab or replaced the Settings tab instead of opening a new one.

bug_report.summary: “Privacy Notice” link styling/focus rendering changed after conversion to html:a (appears visually inconsistent)
steps_to_reproduce: Scroll to the “Firefox/Nightly Data Collection and Use” section.
Press Tab until the “Privacy Notice” link is focused.
expected_behaviors: The “Privacy Notice” link text and focus indicator should render consistently with the pre-change UI (no extra underline/border artifacts, no misalignment).
actual_behaviors: The “Privacy Notice” link’s rendering differs from before (detected changes across the link text area and underline/focus region), suggesting altered underline/focus styling compared to the pre-change appearance.

bug_report.summary: Activating “Privacy Notice” from about:preferences#privacy no longer opens in a new tab; it replaces the Settings tab
steps_to_reproduce: Scroll to the “Firefox/Nightly Data Collection and Use” section.
Focus the “Privacy Notice” link via keyboard (Tab).
Press Enter to activate the link.
expected_behaviors: The “Privacy Notice” link should open the Mozilla privacy page in a new tab, keeping the Settings (about:preferences) tab open (matching prior behavior).
actual_behaviors: The Mozilla privacy page opens by navigating the current Settings tab away from about:preferences, and the original Settings tab is no longer present (only the privacy page tab remains).
